European Works Councils Directive (2009/38/EC) — mandatory cross-border worker information and consultation in multinationals, triggered by the 1997 Renault Vilvoorde plant closure scandal
Blue Card Directive (2009/50/EC) — EU work permit for highly skilled third-country nationals, the EU's attempt to compete with the US H-1B visa and reverse European brain drain
Nuclear Safety Directive (2009/71/EURATOM, revised 2014/87/EURATOM) — binding EU nuclear safety framework, substantially strengthened after the 2011 Fukushima Daiichi disaster
Succession Regulation (EU 650/2012) — which country's law governs cross-border inheritance within the EU, ending legal chaos for millions of expatriates and mixed-nationality families
Rome I Regulation (EC 593/2008) — choice of law for contractual obligations across the EU, replacing the 1980 Rome Convention and completing the single market for contract law
Rome II Regulation (EC 864/2007) — choice of law for non-contractual obligations (torts, unjust enrichment) across the EU, unifying rules that had fragmented across 27 national systems
Access to Documents Regulation (EC 1049/2001) — public right of access to European Parliament, Council and Commission documents, born from the Maastricht legitimacy crisis and Danish 'No' vote
EU Global Human Rights Sanctions Regime (EU 2020/1998) — the EU Magnitsky Act, allowing targeted sanctions against individuals responsible for serious human rights violations worldwide

Students and Researchers Directive (EU 2016/801) — conditions for entry and residence of third-country nationals for study, research, and training, part of the EU's knowledge economy and brain-circulation agenda
